parameters.content:
## Nexus OS — Approval Trigger → Channel Sender

Receives the POST that `app/api/approval/route.ts` fires on **Approve** and forwards it to the
typed, tenant-scoped send executor `POST /api/internal/n8n/send-reply` (docs/channel_sender.md).

**Thin by design:** all send logic (decrypt, refresh, transport, idempotency, status writes)
lives in the Next.js executor. This workflow only validates and forwards. n8n NEVER decrypts a
token or talks to Gmail.

**n8n env required:**
- `NEXUS_APP_BASE_URL` — base URL of the Next.js app (e.g. https://app.nexus… ) 
- `N8N_INGEST_TOKEN` — bearer token for `/api/internal/n8n/*` (server-only)

**DO NOT ACTIVATE** until: (1) send-reply tests pass, and (2) Gmail `gmail.send` scope is
provisioned on the production domain (currently read-only → transport 403). Import for review.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: true, forwarded: true, executor: $json }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: true, skipped: true, reason: $json.reason }) }}
